symbols: cherub heads — the angelic presences that attend the divine, representing innocence and heavenly joy
wings barely visible — the celestial nature of these beings
description: A chalk drawing on toned paper showing two cherub heads in close proximity. The left cherub faces left in three-quarter profile, with curly hair and a small visible wing. The right cherub faces downward with eyes closed, also with curly hair, pressing close to the first. Both have plump, childlike features rendered in soft chalk with white highlights. The drawing has a warm, golden-brown paper tone. It is a study sketch with fine, delicate modeling.
